title: Expand subitems as default view
excerpt: Is there a way to keep subitems expanded when navigating away from a board and then returning? Seems like there should be a default setting or a view that could be saved to avoid having to re-expand every time.

posts.author: nasmith (Nicole Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to keep subitems expanded when navigating away from a board and then returning? Seems like there should be a default setting or a view that could be saved to avoid having to re-expand every time.

posts.author: destinygozero (Destiny)
posts.cookedHtml: YES! I just came on here to look for the answer to this question. Almost all of our boards have subitems and it is frustrating and a waste of time each time we reopen a board to have to go in and click each item to expand each group of subitems! I am hoping someone has a soluation!

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @nasmith and @destinygozero! Thanks for posting about this

I can definitely understand how it would be helpful to leave subitems expanded and would prevent unnecessary clicks/re-expansion. I would recommend posting feature request for this here! Submit an Idea - monday Community Forum

posts.author: mbenerofe (Mark Benerofe)
posts.cookedHtml: When is this feature being added?

posts.author: jenstratton (Jen Stratton)
posts.cookedHtml: Am new to Community, and want to make sure that I vote in the right place because I want this feature too!

posts.author: ShannaKaeM (Shanna)
posts.cookedHtml: It is a bummer that we can not expand all subitems as a view. Is this feature being added? Or is there a workaround?

posts.author: jwrathall (Josh Wrathall)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature. I have over 100 items on a board, each with subitems, and I want to be able to scan through all subitems as tasks as an option on a board.

posts.author: lanceseabourn (Lance Seabourn)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please! Upvote! We have opening checklist for our clinic managers and need to make it as easy as possible. Using a seperate column for each task wasn’t feasible (due to the volume of tasks)

posts.author: AndyKim (Donggyu Kim)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please! My team1 have to generate 5-10 items a day, and team 2~4 should open each 30-50 subtaasks a day. What a loss!

posts.author: Annie (Annie OReilly)
posts.cookedHtml: This is driving me absolutely nuts as there isn’t even a clear visual indicator that your item has any subitems so I keep thinking I’ve forgotten to add sub-items and then realising I have already. Please add this!!!

posts.author: TWX (twx@monkedao.io)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s been a year since this was mentioned, just set the sub tasks as expanded by default instead of the opposite!

posts.author: Morne (Morne du Preez)
posts.cookedHtml: @TWX Hi. Can you kindly advise where on Monday.com I have to go to set the sub tasks to be expanded by default?

posts.author: Deemah (Deemah Ramadan)
posts.cookedHtml: New to the community too. Did this get added as a feature?

posts.author: Morne (Morne du Preez)
posts.cookedHtml: No, not that I’m aware of This is a big inconvenience.

posts.author: Robert.Mauro (Robert Mauro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please, add this feature. This would literally be a reason why we decide to choose another product for our team. I have too many projects I manage to have to expand the sub-items every time I log in or refresh or change anything.

@TRB-monday.com is there an update? It’s been over a year now.

posts.author: olgak (olga kostina)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @TRB-monday.com , adyen really needs this to improve internal communications

posts.author: Anguss (Angus)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE ADD THIS FEATURE, IF ALREADY ADDED, PLEASE LET US KNOW ON THIS FORUM.

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be a welcome addition to the sub items functionality. We also have a lot of parent items with sub items that are assigned to members of our team. The need to constantly toggle expand on all the items with each board refresh is now becoming cumbersome for our team. As others have re-iterated, the option to save a view on all sub items expanded or just saving this state by default on the board would be a great addition.

Thanks

posts.author: AmandaMorgan (Amanda MOrgan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All! I just voted for this - I believe this option is absolutely vital and for many reasons! As well, I created a new suggestion that you can vote for as well if you like (URL below). If any of you know of additional posts on this topic that I can vote for please let me know. I’d spend a whole day voting for each one of them!

Subitems: Add the option to expand/collapse all at once Feature requests
It would be extremely useful to add the option to expand/collapse all subitems on a board at once. It’s been my experience that sometimes tasks are overlooked which has resulted in missed deadlines. As a bit of additional information, we consistently add temporary guests to some of our boards who are assigned tasks, and even though we explain that they need to look for Subitems they are sometimes missed anyway. This would also be extremely useful to our internal users (i.e. those who manage th…

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TRB-monday.com, do we have any movement on this yet or insight if it’s being considered or not? We really need the ability to set the view to be toggled by default.

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Does anyone know if click up allows this? We went for Monday originally but been seeing a lot of love for click up lately… Not sure what their dev team is like at adding new features and fixing bugs but Monday doesn’t really have any excuses with the amount of revenue they are making atm. How many times do we come across simple things that should have been implemented years ago but they haven’t. Yet the price keeps going up. Don’t be surprised if they offer this feature for enterprise only. Or like with native Monday forms were promised more features but now they have created a new product “workforms” that requires a whole new subscription.

posts.author: nancibee (NancyBaunis)
posts.cookedHtml: Why does Monday keep ignoring the needs of their user community? Not only having to expand subitems negatively impacting productivity when working on the board, it is annoying to senior management members who want to review the board.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

Firstly, we apologise to hear of your frustration and want to assure you that we are doing our best to advocate for these feature requests, especially those with a significant amount of votes. We are actively working on our responsiveness, and recognise that there is certainly room for improvement, so appreciate your patience with us. Just for some background, we are in the process of merging threads that reference the same feature request so that votes are consolidated and the feature can be better prioritised - this particular request being one of them. Your needs and requests are not being ignored, there is much happening behind the scenes, however we do acknowledge we can be more efficient and transparent in our approach, and promise we are working on this!

posts.author: Gurrier (Gareth)
posts.cookedHtml: Ive been looking through this feature request forum, there isnt a single comment from Monday on specific queries. I think thats your problem, this copy and pasted stuff doesnt really help.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Gurrier - I’ll definitely take your feedback on board!

As mentioned, we are in the process of merging duplicate requests so that we can pull an accurate pool of votes together to get highly requested features like these considered and developed in the near future. As such, providing specific updates for some feature isn’t as easy or possible, as we want to ensure we have all the data gathered before publicly providing an update that is confirmed and completely accurate - we understand that there are topics that have been requested a significant time back and we do apologise for this. Our team is doing their best to advocate for these features, and have them implemented as efficiently as possible for the community whilst ensuring performance is of the highest standard - we hope you can understand! Because of this (^) we try to maintain consistent and honest communication with you all, however I hear you and will note your comments.

Once we have specifics to provide, you will know about it!

posts.author: Robert.Mauro (Robert Mauro)
posts.cookedHtml:
I hope your dev team also ranks by simplicity. This is low hanging fruit. One button to trigger the appropriate action on one class should do this, if a master class is being used on the sub-item groups.

Regardless, pretty please, can we see this done?

Monday was really great at being at the forefront when we started testing and then using it, but, other project/time manager platforms are going beyond, including with auto expandable sub-items, deep nested sub items and better “smart actions” by default.

I’ve moved a big chunk of my team to Monday, and hope to be able to get back to the point where it’s once again state of the art.

I do understand that integrations are VERY important to the success of Monday, and the usefulness of it to us, but, if the underlying platform stagnates, that doesn’t matter very much.

posts.author: corymohrdesign (Cory Mohr)
posts.cookedHtml: Just adding my vote. This DEFINITELY needs to be an option!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nasmith

Great idea and thanks for sharing your feedback. We’ve discussed the request to expand subitems as default views with the relevant development and they agree with the need for this. As of now, it’s not immediately planned, due to other priorities and team capacity.

We would love to keep hearing how this feature would impact your workflow. Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our users care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback makes a difference.

Have another idea? Submit it here.

posts.author: TamaraLove (Tamara Love)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would love to see expand and collapse functionality added to subtasks - as it stands, as soon as you navigate away from a board, the subtasks all collapse into the parent task.
Would love to have an option to keep these open until collapsed back manually. Also, keyboard shortcuts to achieve this would be perfect .

posts.author: TixFAN (Ken Lowson)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that seems like a simple fix to a very at the least annoying visual issue.

posts.author: agencyaccess (Keith Gentile)
posts.cookedHtml: Ya I just spent about 3 hours setting up a new board to use sub tasks but without a way to open and close them all at the same time it’s really no use to me.

posts.author: smatz (Scott Matz)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this feature, or a more obvious way to indicate there are subtasks beyond the small column. We have users who very easily overlook the items when glancing at rows.

posts.author: Robert.Mauro (Robert Mauro)
posts.cookedHtml:
PLEASE implement this feature. Every time I change boards, everything collapses again.

  • Expand ALL sub-items in all groups
  • Remember view/settings

Pretty please.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

Firstly, we’d just like to sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, as we pull together all the votes associated with this topic, we would love if you can redirect the votes on this thread, to the primary thread (most voted) here: Expand subitems as default view - as we plan to close this smaller thread in an attempt to consolidate all votes, and better prioritise this feature request based upon the total amount of votes

posts.author: AmandaMorgan (Amanda MOrgan)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be extremely useful to add the option to expand/collapse all subitems on a board at once. It’s been my experience that sometimes tasks are overlooked which has resulted in missed deadlines. As a bit of additional information, we consistently add temporary guests to some of our boards who are assigned tasks, and even though we explain that they need to look for Subitems they are sometimes missed anyway.

This would also be extremely useful to our internal users (i.e. those who manage the boards) to be able to expand all subitems at once as it would provide much greater ease to view everything at once instead of having to click each one - especially when we are exporting to Excel. I have spoken with many of our other internal users and everyone agrees that it would be super helpful to add this option.

posts.author: Dominic (Dominic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday Team,

It’s been great playing around with this!

My top 3 requests so far:

  1. Unique pulse IDs for each new sub item (to allow for Integromat integrations)
  2. Column summaries (to allow for currency pre-fixes, sums, medians etc)
  3. Sub items to maintain the same expanded/collapsed state after returning from another board (it’s what my mind expects to see)

Thanks
Dominic

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Issues I’ve encountered:

  1. Unable to drag and drop subitems to other items - even though the subitem follows the mouse as you drag it.
  2. Right-click > Duplicate. The green success box appears but nothing is duplicated.
  3. Right-click > Copy+Paste does nothing

posts.author: johan.n (Johan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looking good!
It would be great if the sub-items’ values could be summarized in the main item.
For example, time estimates and spent worktime on sub-items would be convenient to see as one single value in the main item.

Some kind of connection with status columns would also be great. Maybe the same visualization as the checkmarks could be used to indicate (in the main item) how many sub-items that have status X.

I was also thinking about the kanban view. A toggle that could hide/show sub-items as separate cards might be good.

posts.author: Dominic (Dominic)
posts.cookedHtml: For the most flexibility, I’d say that it’s better not to summarise the sub-items values in the main item. I think that the sub-items should be discrete items in their own right, but with column summaries if needed.

posts.author: SamChristophers (Sam Christophers)
posts.cookedHtml:
No sure how realistic/implementable this is, but for the way we work, it would be good to have ‘linked boards’ per item.

We have an ‘overview’ board of all of our upcoming jobs, organised into groups per month. For instance, March has 10 items, April 5 and so on. We then have boards that relate to these specific jobs, which break down the content/work we need to do for them further.

With the use of these subtask, on our main board it would be ideal if we could LINK to various board, as opposed to only being able to link to one other board for the whole overview. I know we could set up multiple link columns, but that isn’t an ideal workaround. Ideally, what I would like to do is to be able to set up SUBITEMS within a PARENT ITEM that has a link to one of our more details boards. Each item, ideally, would be able to link to another board, without issue

posts.author: guillemarqueta (Guillermo Marqueta)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! Great job with sub-items!!

My feedback with the friction I felt starting with it:

  1. Move parent items and its subitems to another board
  2. When filtering by a person, be able to show the parent item if this person is only present at a sub-item (helps us visualize a bigger picture of what she is working on)
  3. Copy columns (specially status ones) from parent items to sub-items. I had to create them again from scratch.
  4. Convert one item into a sub-item of another one and viceversa
  5. Through Google Chrome, move from fields with keyboard arrows as in parent items

Thanks for moving on!

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our biggest usage hope for subitems was to be able to remedy printing boards in a concise fashion. Currently all boards seem to default the item name column (farthest left) to fill the width which can not be modified shorter than a set amount, so our boards are unable to fit well on a printed 8.5" x 11" sheet. The only remedy I could find was to scale down the size to sub-90%, which is too small to read. My hope with sub-items was that I could put notes for each delivery (our use case is a delivery schedule for a dumpster rental company) in the subitems and they could nest nicely below each item, but by default there is just way too much extra vertical space being added for sub-items. My hopes would be for some additional view/printing settings to adjust font size/padding of cells, etc. This is also a feature I’ve requested in the past for utilizing Form View on external websites. The padding of cells looks great onscreen for a board, but it’s just too large for other uses. See my screenshots:

Fitting-All-Columns-on-Letter-Sized-Print-Out-Doesnt-Fit-Unless-Unreadably-Small-at-60-Percent-Blurred999×660 73.2 KB
Item-Name-Column-Defaulting-Extreme-Width-Blurred1780×336 96.8 KB
Sub-items-with-Excessive-Vertical-Space-Blurred1774×274 47.1 KB
Sub-items-with-Print-Dialog-Options-on-Mac-Blurred997×657 64.4 KB

posts.author: Chris_Mech_Eng (Chris Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
In general, I love the sub-items.

My main point of feedback is it would be nice to have a standard set of sub-items that could be auto-generated for each new item. For example on certain boards, there’s a standard list of tasks that need to be accomplished for all items (which often are forgotten). This equates to about 6 sub-items that need to be manually generated for each item on that board. Is this something that already exists and I’ve overlooked it?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is what we need too…
a standard template of subitems to be created (via automation??) por a type of pulse.
example:
i have a “type of task” status column, i could have an automation to create a specific list of subitems for this task, and another set of subitems for another type of tasks

posts.author: streety (James Street)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be great to have the subitem columns replicate the same as the main item. I have to go across all of my boards and mimic the columns for the main item, for example…

  • Main item:
    Example campaign

  • Subitems:
    Social media posts
    eShot assets

These would all need columns for job owner, designer, type, deadline, content, estimated time

posts.author: ashleyg (Ashley)
posts.cookedHtml:
johan.n:

For example, time estimates and spent worktime on sub-items would be convenient to see as one single value in the main item.

My team requested this as well! I’m sort of wondering how it will impact how time tracking displays in the Dashboards, though…

posts.author: rawagschal (Rachel Wagschal)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team uses Monday.com for scheduling social media posts, so our use of the mobile app is frequent. We have just started implementing use of sub-items which has been very helpful on desktop for organizing posts by topic (main item) and platform (sub-items), yet have run into the problem of not being able to view sub-items on mobile. In short:

  • Full view of sub-items on mobile

posts.author: Ben_D (Benjamin Danneberg)
posts.cookedHtml: Can items and their subitems be duplicated in the future?

posts.author: lost_gweedo (Will Strand)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for sub-items. Currently, I have a custom list in the status column. It would be nice if that custom list would be available for sub items also. Currently, I have to go into the status column of a sub item and edit the list to match the status list of the parent item.
Thanks!

posts.author: bglover (Brandi Glover)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to echo the feedback on a request for Subitems to maintain the same expanded/collapsed state after returning from another board.

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are working to implement Sub Items, however I am not so sure I want to grant other users full access to the board in board permission just to allow them to add sub items (Edit Everything).

You can add new pulses with the “Edit Content” permission, but not add sub items…the permissions are not consistent in regards to creating pulses and sub items.

Please consider to change so pulses and sub items work using the same permission.

posts.author: Scragemi (Emily Scragg)
posts.cookedHtml: We need to be able to promote or demote items into subitems (drop and drag) or vice versa

posts.author: erics (Eric Siegert)
posts.cookedHtml: Suggestion: What about an Item Level Progress Bar that is connected to the sub-item categories - but not from left to right, but from top to the last one in one category? Like a mirror for a checklist. The feedback for the status on the sub-items is missing otherwise on the top level.

posts.author: KH123456 (Kylie)
posts.cookedHtml: A suggestion for subtasks is to have them carry over to a board built from a template. I added them to one of our templates then tried to copy that tempate into a new board and none of the subtasks populated on the board.

posts.author: Whitfield (Whit Gurley)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here! Loving sub-items, but it’s tiring having to re-open those trees whenever you return to a board. Remember the trees, please!

Edit: And it would also be nice to have a feature to open/close all sub-items on a board.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Whitfield:

Edit: And it would also be nice to have a feature to open/close all sub-items on a board.

Yes! I have often wished there was an Open All Subitems/Close All Subitems button.

posts.author: Demelza (Demelza)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would really like to see an option on the board where you can set the subitems to take on all the same columns as the parent items.

Would also love to be able to promote (drag and drop) a sub item to a pulse and vice versa.

We would also like an ‘open all’ or 'close all" subitems options for the board.

We also really need to be able to access subitems on the mobile app to use this functionality.

posts.author: Amy.K (Amy K Bottom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, loving subitems so far, but we’ve hit a couple of snags (My Week and mobile not being forthcoming with what project the subitem was part of, inability to save a template of subitems) which I see already mentioned. There are a few other things that would make subitems much more useful for us.

We have 20-30 projects at a given with basically the same phases and steps for each, and while it has streamlined our process considerably to have those 23 steps in subitems on the main board instead of referring back to individual project boards for each (yikes), being able to group those tasks into phases would solve so many issues, especially with the new automation that notices when a group is marked “done.” A way to group subitems, followed by a way to change a status in the parent item when a group is completed would be amazing.

I was also wondering about labels for units of measurement for the subitem number columns and a way to aggregate the mirrored sum with the numbers in the parent item for budget breakdowns. Mirroring onto the parent is spiffy, but I wish I could roll it into the formula.

I look forward to seeing what y’all cook up next!

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,

Thank you all for continuing to submit your feedback on subitems (positive and negative). We know there are still a lot of things missing and as we mentioned these are going to be released in increments. This is not only make sure that everything is stable, but to also ensure designed properly and functioning as anticipated.

We are continuing to monitor this thread and pass along all feedback to our product team, so please continue to share!

If at any point you experience any bugs or unusual behavior please write into support@monday.com!

Thank you and stay safe all

posts.author: Eleanor (Eleanor Holmes)
posts.cookedHtml:
My feedback:

  1. Subitems should inherit the same column setup as the pulse they’re attached to. Mine are being spawned with simply Owner, Status and Date and are ignoring all the other columns set up on that board. This needs to include any custom setup of the status column. At present, when you create a sub-item you have to reconfigure all your columns for the subitem. This does seem to be a one-off deal thankfully, in that all future subitems on that board pick up the same setup, but it’s still very painful if you have a complicated column setup.

  2. We need to be able to drag a pulse into or under another pulse to make it a subitem - without that feature, you can’t use Monday as a braindump, because you have plan your tasks as you’re writing them, instead of being able to order and organise them once you’re done. See Omnifocus for a great example of how this functionality could/should work.

  3. We need to be able to nest sub-items, otherwise it’s not actually letting us organise our work sequentially, it’s just catering to one particular style of task organisation. Obviously there needs to be a limit just for useability’s sake, but being able to nest several layers deep is important.

  4. The interface for subitems is very disruptive to the view of one’s task list as a whole. With subitem lists showing, it chews up a lot of unnecessary whitespace above and below the subitem list, making it way harder to see the state of one’s work at a glance.

I’m sure there would be disagreement here from some users but I’d far prefer to have the subitem columns aligned with the main pulse columns, and just have the subitem name/label column indented and some visual distinction; that way you wouldn’t need the whitespace above and below, and it’d be a lot more useable.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: I think your first point “Subitems should inherit the same column setup as the pulse they’re attached to” must be left to user choice or in board configuration (keep main pulse columns structure when creating subitems on/off). Subitems its a good way to make our main items structure short and showing the resume of the subitems data.

posts.author: tweigman (Tom Weigman)
posts.cookedHtml: When going through your inbox, the title of the entry in your inbox doesn’t have any mention of the pulse it’s a subitem of, requiring you to click through to the pulse in order to see what it’s referring to. this makes it much more difficult to go through your inbox for status updates as you have no idea what pulse the update for.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hlopezvc @Eleanor great feedback from you both! This is actually something we are looking into (i.e. the ability to choose whether or not to have a separate structure or to inherit the parent structure)!

An updated roadmap should be coming next week with more details and more specific timelines.

Be on the lookout in this thread

posts.author: Whitfield (Whit Gurley)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey guys, I just discovered a bug: on boards that use subitems, the expanded/collapsed states of regular pulse groups aren’t retained when switching to another board.

posts.author: dsscoe (D. Scott Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brett!

Did the updated roadmap get released? My team is waiting for the ability to duplicate boards with sub items in tact. Any status on this one? I understand this is in active development now.

Thanks!

posts.author: jsararas (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for maintain state as well as open/close all.

posts.author: ebless-batomic (Eliot Bless)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, would love an update on this one. Not including the ability to duplicate subitems severely hampers the use of the tool for repeatable software implementation projects.

posts.author: colin (Colin Healy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Dominic! Love the sub-items. One small thing I’m finding is I’m unable to “shift+select” a long list of subitems like you can with the main items on the board.

See video for more detailed explanation: Video Here

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Another thing to consider is to see Subitems in the Board shared views.
Actually i share a board view with Subitems and it doesnt display them.

posts.author: JamesWW (James Wood)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sub-items are a game changer for my team, so thanks first of all!

As well as some of the QOL changes above like keeping expanded/collapsed state, the main thing for us would be some kind of summary at the main item level. For example if you can set a progress column on the main item to pick up completion statuses of all subitems under the main item (top to bottom).

posts.author: Jorma (Jorma Härm)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @brett-monday.com

[BUG] Multiple sub-item duplication

Didn’t see it posted so I’ll share my bug.

  1. Created Item
  2. Created 34 sub-items
  3. Duplicated 22 of those sub-items
  4. Refresh/Move between boards will REMOVE all duplicated sub-items

Even when I renamed the duplicated items, they still disappeared after switching between boards.
They weren’t in the Recycle Bin, they just disappear.
Duplicate Items and Duplicate Items with Updates both disappear.

Duplicating only 3 items works ok, so I guess it glitches at a bigger bulk of sub-items.

posts.author: jpekovitch (Jason Pekovitch)
posts.cookedHtml: Are there plans to show subtasks on other views / dashboards. My first use case was to use subtasks for resource allocation (this would provide the ability to have resources with different hour allocations) but subtasks are not accounted for on the workload view. I was also considering using them for tasks inside a project phase but they do not show on the timeline either. That one may not be an intended use, I’m currently using linked items for this.

posts.author: jiminy (Jim Hourican)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dominic:
  • Column summaries (to allow for currency pre-fixes, sums, medians etc)

We totally need this capability as well. For example, I use subitems for each payment on a Purchase Order. I’d love to see a summary total in dollar format.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Aksalano:

permiss

Couldnt agree more, it doesnt make any sense to assign someone to the subitems if the permissions are set to an assigned user on the main item. If i need to make permissions more flexible i will be allowing anyone to edit or make changes on any pulse when its not required.

Besides this i see another problems:

  1. Notifications: There is not reference to the main item that this subitem depends, they say “you are owner of the subitem of the board subitems of BOARD NAME” i think there is an error… the name of the main pulse is missing here, because its the more important data of reference.

  2. Email notifications when im assigned to a subitem: As i was saying with notifications, the same happens in the email notifications, there is no reference to the main item that this subitems depends… This is critical.

  3. Android app subitems management: they are supported but as i was saying there is no reference, button, there is no way to go back and see from what main item i was assigned…

All this items should be fixed in order to comunicate well with other users.

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: After missing a bunch of deadlines this week, I have realised why! Subtasks do not appear in My Week when using the “Show Only Deadlines” mode. This is a huge gap right now.

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d like to be able to move items in and out of other items. Sometimes multiple items get created that, on review, all belong to one larger task. In that case we’d like to create an item to be that larger task then move the individual items to be sub-items of it. Not only would it be annoying to have to recreate those items as sub-items, any conversation had on the original items would be lost.

posts.author: andrewm (Andrew markwell)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking good so far - It would be good if subitems columns are exposed to the formula column, so that we can aggregate number values on the parent item.

posts.author: rafaeltrapp (Rafael Trapp)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great if sub-items columns are exposed on board “filter everything” feature, showing only sub-itens filtered as result.

posts.author: CWaxman (CaraWaxman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi - it would be great to be able to expand sub-items when on a table dashboard view.

posts.author: KellyM (Kelly Morrin)
posts.cookedHtml: I know others have mentioned, but for our team, as much as we’re loving sub-items, we are really limited by not being able to move tasks with sub-items between boards, exporting them to Excel, and the parent item not being shown when searching. That last one in particular is maddening - you search for your own name and you see a sea of sub-items listed and no clue what they are for. Can’t wait for these functionalities to be released!

posts.author: NoChance (Chance)
posts.cookedHtml:
Export Board to include SubItems

Usecase: Subitems are used to track multiple telecom inventory disconnect requests and billing disputes per location. As part of the process, we need to gather all Telecom provided tracking numbers across their multiple systems including forked requests. The forked requests are out of our control and creates a Many to One relationship that SubItems is perfect for.

Without the ability to export SubItems, we have to either manually retype everything in the Exported Spreadsheet or figure out a work around.

Thanks!

posts.author: LaurelOnco360 (Laurel)
posts.cookedHtml: One of the biggest asks for our company as well is to be able to drag an email pulse into a subitem. This is something we have struggled with since starting to use Monday. We need to have a way to drag an email pulse that gets sent to the main board into an existing item without emailing that specific item. This is causing a major snag for us and a duplication of work. We were hopeful subitems would solve this problem but sadly no. If we had this ability we would utilize Monday so much more.

posts.author: nviusgraphics (Robert Reed)
posts.cookedHtml: I second the need for Column Summaries for the sub items. I would like to use the columns to calculate a sum and allow for the use of currency.

Hi Monday Team! Great work on the subitems features.

posts.author: jpekovitch (Jason Pekovitch)

posts.cookedHtml: I’ve recently noticed that adding a new subitem to a list of subitems automatically places it in the 3rd row as opposed to at the bottom row, is there a way of changing this so that the newest subitems always appear below the older ones?

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Chris, I see this post is quite old, but I thought I’d mention that we have the same use case, and we have set up custom automations to auto create subitems when items move from 1 stage to another. The same would work on item creation. Perhaps you have moved on or already figured this out, but I found it super helpful to auto create subitems. Hope it helps!

Cheers!

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
I Second the feature request to be able to filter by the subitem columns. - something like:

  • filter by subitem column
  • if filter matches show parent item and subitems where criteria matches.

posts.author: elena (elena)
posts.cookedHtml: Item card different for subitem and parent item. We like the item card and use it quite a lot. But if i design a item card for the parent, this item card is visible in the subitem but not wiht the columns of the parent but the subitem. It does not have much sense to have for example an item card called “customer details” that refferes to the parent and show data of the subitem that does not have information of customer. Will appreciate different item cards. or at least if i choose to see the item card designed for the parent to have the data from the parent.

posts.author: risqybusiness (Jennifer Outcelt)
posts.cookedHtml: I would LOVE this feature in subitems! I often need to update many subitems at once and being able to “shift + select” them would save me sooo much clicking. Please make this possible!

posts.author: nolanhausler (Nolan Hausler)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for this tip @timlittletech! I just created an automation to do this upon item creation. Would be great to have as a standard option but the workaround is good.

posts.author: CJEStaff (Jodi Portnoy)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been a roadmap published or a suggestion to the need to have sub-items have the same columns as the parent items? I am looking to create groups and need to be able to convert my items to subitems but don’t want to lose the data in my column setup and prefer not to have to recreate the whole thing.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

We sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, we did just want to mention that some of the feature requests here are now supported:

  • Mirroring subitem data into the parent item:

Screen Shot 2022-12-07 at 11.14.20 am1544×724 79.9 KB

  • Filtering by subitems is supported in our advanced filtering
  • Bulk selecting subitems is supported by selecting shift and clicking the first and last subitem!

As for maintaining expanded/collapsed view of subitems we encourage you to cast your vote to the primary thread here Expand subitems as default view and please do the same for the request to have subitems inherent parent item data - Subitem inherit column values from Parent - #5 by cerkezi - we plan on closing out this thread, so we have all the votes in one place to better prioritise these feature requests based upon the total amount of votes

Thank you!

posts.author: Robert.Mauro (Robert Mauro)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT

It’s been over two years - is there any update? I only ask because the topic is marked as “solved” and voting is closed.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Robert.Mauro - we are really sorry about this! The feature request was accidentally marked as solved, however should now be open for our community members to continue voting. As for an update, the feature request is currently under consideration and whilst it’s not immediately planned due to other priorities and team capacity, the team certainly agrees there is a need for this

posts.author: ripken (Jeff Kramer)
posts.cookedHtml: Bianca, please consider moving this feature up in the priority list. Our teams are wasting a tremendous amount of time having to click to open up all the subitems every time they open up our Project Workspace. Thanks.

posts.author: tigger2000ttfn (Michael )
posts.cookedHtml: Really Want and Need this feature - We plan on using this in a production environment. Should have the option to auto expand all sub-task by default in every view including Kanban. This is a feature (we must have before we decide to implement this software) this would be a very big enterprise account. Our BioManufacturing techs need to quickly scan the boards and minimize interaction with the touch screen monitors.

posts.author: brazenTasks (Marlon N)
posts.cookedHtml: As default view, or as an option for custom views. It is so annoying to expand sub-items all the time. My organization uses sub-items for every project because there are many tasks assigned to different people for one given project. Our board is set up with groups for each stage a project might be in. Each item is a project, with subitems for the tasks.

posts.author: sburgos1023 (Sara Burgos)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a needed feature… please add!

posts.author: Duncan-AS (Duncan Shotton)
posts.cookedHtml: Pleaaaaaaase make it so Monday.com team!

posts.author: Joe_Downey (Joe Downey)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com @CSG @CXS @monday-team @monday-moderators Can we expect this feature any time soon?

posts.author: JackieGlavind (Jackie Glavind)
posts.cookedHtml: Literally just discussing this feature again this afternoon with a colleague… can’t believe we are still waiting for this!

posts.author: j0shParr (Josh Parr)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely could use this if implemented, thanks!

posts.author: betterton (SB)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely needed as a toggleable option. On some boards we need subtasks hidden, but they are very useful on other boards and need to be visible always, especially since the indicator of subitems is on the less visible side. Alternately, I’d even prefer it if the option was moved out of the hover meatball menu and onto a button. Upper management need to be able to see things at a glance without too many steps.

posts.author: palmerijosh (Josh Palmeri)
posts.cookedHtml: Bump! Please add this feature.

posts.author: Russella2 (Russell Alberson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have to admit. After working with a free trial version of Monday.com the lack of support and the fact that they completely ignore requests for simple features is frustrating as hell. I really do not understand why anyone would use this software. I mean really, expand/collapse all takes two years to implement? Unbelievable
.

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT,

Firstly, I would like to express my gratitude to you and the developers for your hard work in releasing new features and keeping the community informed.

However, and I hope you don’t take this the wrong way, there have been some concerns about how this particular feature has been handled. It was initially reported back in January 2021. After garnering 187 votes, 110 comments, and almost three years of waiting, one would expect a more urgent response.

Regrettably, it has been nine months since we received any significant update on the status of this feature, aside from it being under consideration. While the Monday.com team acknowledges the need for it, there appears to be little progress in its implementation.

Monday.com has become a crucial asset for our company, and the sub-items feature, in particular, is integral to our daily operations. However, constantly toggling these sub-items to expand and collapse upon refreshing or switching boards is cumbersome and inefficient. The absence of a shortcut command to quickly toggle sub-items for each group is surprising, especially when such a shortcut exists for toggling groups.

This situation serves as a persistent reminder of an unfinished feature. Given that we, along with many others, pay for a Monday.com subscription each month, we naturally expect a higher level of service. While I understand the voting system in place to guide engineers in prioritizing features, it is disheartening to see new features being released without the completion of existing ones.

I hope this message helps bring attention back to this issue, and I would kindly request you and the team to mark this on the roadmap. Additionally, if possible, providing a meaningful update on when we can expect the release of this feature would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

posts.author: daniel.jarolim (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature +1.

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Anthony, I could not agree more, especially about the shortcuts. Monday is incredibly mouse driven and this would be a huge relief.

Anthony Woods:

Monday.com has become a crucial asset for our company, and the sub-items feature, in particular, is integral to our daily operations. However, constantly toggling these sub-items to expand and collapse upon refreshing or switching boards is cumbersome and inefficient. The absence of a shortcut command to quickly toggle sub-items for each group is surprising, especially when such a shortcut exists for toggling groups.

posts.author: jennifermead (Jennifer Mead)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello. My team relies on Monday.com for project management, but we’ve encountered a hurdle with the subitem feature. The default collapsing of subitems has led us to abandon their use, as the constant need to expand them makes our workflow less efficient.

Please introduce a feature that allows users to set subitems to remain expanded by default. This adjustment would streamline our workflow and encourage us to utilize the subitem feature for improved organization fully.

Thank you!

posts.author: Sdekis (Sterling Dekis)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvoting! This would be an amazing feature to improve the tedious task of clicking each item to view the sub-items in the group.

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! Also adding in a keyboard shortcut to expand/collapse sub-items would make this much easier as well.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
I am excited to share this feature has gone from “under consideration” to “on roadmap!” After reading the feedback from the community and exploring different development possibilities, we will be expanding subitems capabilities. While we are planning on adding this ability, we do not have an exact date in 2024 of when this feature will be available for use. Thanks again for your thoughful feedback and I look forward to sharing more updates!

Stay tuned and make sure to turn on notifications for this topic to be the first to know when it is released.

posts.author: burstfadehc (Burst Fade Haircuts)
posts.cookedHtml: YES! I just came on here to look for the answer to this question. Almost all of our boards have subitems and it is frustrating and a waste of time each time we reopen a board to have to go in and click each item to expand each group of subitems! I am hoping someone has a soluation!

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: Excellent news!!!

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb Will the development for this include the ability to open/close sub-tasks using Keyboard shortcuts? This would really enhance the user experience for me.

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Amber here from the product team. I’m very excited to announce that as part of some improvements we made to subitems this quarter, you are now able to:
Expand / Collapse all items at the same time (using either the item menu or a keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+I or Cmnd+I)
Return to this view (of expanded / collapsed subitems) by default when moving back and forth between boards:


All expanded or collapsed subitems remain that way after refreshing - Watch Video

posts.author: Regan (Regan Ludwar)
posts.cookedHtml: This is great! Thanks for including the shortcut!!!

posts.author: Sdekis (Sterling Dekis)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so exciting! Thank you!

posts.author: JerWaffles (Jérémy Waffles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Amber,
Will this shortcut also work in shared views?

posts.author: christel (christel aguila)

It would be great to just be able to send them a link to boards without having to say press ctrl+I if the board is already set as that is the view you want them to have all the time so they dont miss seeing sub items

posts.author: DaleH (Dale Holtzhausen)
posts.cookedHtml:
There should be a way to keep subitems expanded when navigating away from a board and then returning. Seems like there should be a default setting or a view that could be saved to avoid having to re-expand every time one returns to a board.

Expand subitems as default view Feature requests
Is there a way to keep subitems expanded when navigating away from a board and then returning? Seems like there should be a default setting or a view that could be saved to avoid having to re-expand every time.

posts.author: EdGra (Ed)
posts.cookedHtml: No idea how this isn’t already a native thing, seems very daft. Is it not possible?

posts.author: jonathan92591 (Jonathan Thompson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Use case:
As a project manager,
I want to expand all subtasks, so that I can…
I want to collapse all subtasks, so that I can…
Quickly clean up/organize the board based on my immediate needs

Context:
Sometimes when I have many tasks with subtasks I get information overload and want to get back to only seeing the tasks for the whole board.

Thank you!

posts.cookedHtml: Hey Michelle! Shannon here with the monday team. I merged your feature request with this existing one to ensure all related posts are centralized in one place.
